item.title: Govt to expand dialysis services to upazila hospitals: minister
item.description: The government has undertaken an initiative to expand dialysis services to district and upazila hospitals across Bangladesh to improve access to treatment for kidney patients, health minister Sardar Md Sakhawat Hossain said on Monday....

item.title: Govt to decide on Ad-din Hospital after fresh inspection
item.description: Health and family welfare minister Sardar Md Sakhawat Husain on Monday said that the government would take its next decision regarding Ad-din Medical College Hospital after a fresh inspection to verify whether the institution had addressed the deficiencies identified by the authorities....News

item.title: Immunisation to include typhoid vaccine from Aug 1
item.description: The government has decided to introduce the Typhoid Conjugate Vaccine into its routine immunisation programme for children from August 1....

item.title: Students battle floods, heavy rain to sit for exams, attend schools
item.description: Thousands of students across Bangladesh battled floods, heavy rain and waterlogged roads in the past few days to sit for the Higher Secondary Certificate and equivalent examinations and also to attend schools....

item.title: Special attention to state-owned banks warranted
item.description: ALMOST half of total outstanding loans in nine state-owned banks turning non-performing and the little and slow recovery success warrants special attention and efforts. The finance minister, on July 12, told the parliament that defaulted loans at the country’s nine state-owned banks reached Tk 1,88,701 crore by May 31. This means that about 46 per cent of the total......

item.title: Quality education must reach every district
item.description: SUCCESSIVE governments have succeeded in increasing primary school enrolment, but higher enrolment alone has not translated into quality learning, a gap laid bare by the results of the primary scholarship programme. The government-funded scholarship programme recognises high-performing students at the end of primary education and provides recipients with......

item.title: Myanmar tells ASEAN Suu Kyi will be looked after
item.description: The Southeast Asian bloc ASEAN was told on Sunday by Myanmar’s foreign minister that detained former leader Aung San Suu Kyi was ‘a sister’ who was in good health and would be looked after, ASEAN’s special envoy to Myanmar said, reports The Jakarta Post on Monday....ExecutiveBranch

item.title: Preventable losses, repeated failures
item.description: A TWO-YEAR-OLD boy, Ismail Hossain, wandered out of his home in Satkania on a Friday night and drowned in floodwater that had engulfed his neighbourhood. His family could not find a patch of dry ground to bury him, and the funeral was eventually held beside a pond on slightly higher land. A few kilometres away, in Banshkhali, two more children were swept......

item.title: Perils and promises
item.description: ON MAY 7, the Dhaka Metropolitan Police began enforcing traffic law at several of the capital’s busiest intersections without posting an officer at any of them. A network of cameras running the AI-Based Road Transport Act 2018 Violation Detection System now identifies violations, reads number plates against the Bangladesh Road Transport Authority database, and......Country-specificnews

item.title: Revolution still waiting for its republic
item.description: ON THE second anniversary of the July Uprising, Bangladesh confronts an uncomfortable truth. The movement succeeded in removing an authoritarian regime, but it has yet to dismantle the authoritarian state......

item.title: A landmark promise for tomorrow
item.description: Midwifery is not just a healthcare service; it is a lifeline for mothers, newborns and families. Globally, the world faces a staggering deficit of ten million midwives, and Bangladesh is no exception. To truly serve our nation, the country urgently needs a substantial number of well-educated, well-trained and properly recruited midwives to step into communities and save......

item.title: Israeli forces kill man in West Bank
item.description: Israeli forces on Monday shot dead a Palestinian worker as he attempted to climb over a barrier separating Jerusalem from the occupied West Bank, the Palestinian health ministry said......

item.title: Trump reinstates Iran blockade
item.description: President Donald Trump said Monday that the United States was reinstating its blockade of Iranian ports and ‘taking over’ the Strait of Hormuz, where it would levy charges on shipping....

item.title: EU, UK hit Russia with joint sanctions over cyber attacks
item.description: The European Union and Britain targeted Russia Monday with coordinated sanctions over cyber attacks in Europe, accusing Moscow’s FSB intelligence agency over involvement in digital strikes......Maps

item.title: Spanish PM urges better prevention after wildfire
item.description: Prime minister Pedro Sanchez on Monday urged Spain to strengthen its prevention measures after one of the country’s deadliest wildfires in recent history killed 13 people and razed a huge area......

item.title: Lanka’s Tamil minority parties unite to seek greater autonomy
item.description: Sri Lanka’s Tamil-speaking minorities launched a united front on Monday to demand a new constitution granting greater autonomy, reviving long-standing grievances two decades after the end of the island’s long ethnic war......

item.title: Pig’s head found in Bosnian Muslim cemetery
item.description: A pig’s head discovered in a Muslim cemetery in the ethnically divided Bosnian city of Mostar forced the cancellation of a memorial service on Monday, amid heightened tensions in the lead-up to a general election later this year......Death& Tragedy

item.title: JS passes bill allowing BMU to form company for healthcare
item.description: The Jatiya Sangsad on Monday passed the Bangladesh Medical University (Second Amendment) Act, 2026, allowing the university to establish a for-profit or not-for-profit company to deliver healthcare services, a move the government said would improve specialised treatment....

item.description: Doctors Without Borders on Monday condemned what it called Russia’s ‘deliberate strategy to destroy the healthcare system’ in Ukraine, documenting a consistent pattern of attacks throughout the war......

item.title: US vows campaign to end ICC ‘threat’ to Americans
item.description: The United States on Monday announced a campaign against the Hague-based International Criminal Court, accusing the tribunal of posing ‘an intolerable threat to US sovereignty’ and threatening sanctions......

item.description: The parliament on Monday constituted six more parliamentary standing committees on key ministries with ruling BNP lawmakers as chairpersons......News

item.description: Posts, telecommunications and information technology minister Faqir Mahbub Anam on Monday reaffirmed the government’s commitment to ensuring a reliable and investment-friendly environment for foreign investors in Bangladesh. ‘...

item.description: The Jatiya Sangsad on Monday passed the Narcotics Control (Amendment) Act, 2026, introducing specialised tribunals for speedy trial of narcotics-related offences, legal provisions to tackle drug crimes in cyberspace....

item.title: Heavy rains push Kaptai power generation to 206MW
item.description: Electricity generation at the Karnaphuli Hydroelectric Power Station has increased to 206 megawatts as the water level in Kaptai Lake rose due to incessant rainfall and runoff from upstream hilly areas....

item.title: All madrassahs directed to hold events on July Martyrs’ Day
item.description: The Madrassah Education Board has directed all the madrassahs educational institutions of the country to organise commemorative meetings and special prayers on July 16 to observe July Martyrs’ Day 26....ExecutiveBranch

item.description: The Finance Division on Monday requested the Ministry of Public Administration to review the monthly car maintenance allowance enjoyed by certain government officials....

item.description: More than five decades after Bangladesh achieved independence, residents of around 10 villages at Kaliganj in Lalmonirhat are still waiting for a paved road that they say is vital to their daily lives....

item.description: Bangladesh Agricultural Development Corporation chairman Md Azizul Islam has urged officials to deliver essential agricultural services, including quality seeds, non-urea fertilisers and irrigation facilities, directly to farmers doorsteps smoothly and on time....InternationalSports Competitions

item.description: Nine workers were killed at a waste-to-energy plant in western India after a garbage heap collapsed onto an administrative building following heavy rains, the facility's operator said Monday.....

item.description: Dhaka district had the highest number of primary scholarship recipients in the 2025 Primary Scholarship Examinations, while Bandarban district recorded the lowest, according to results published on Sunday....

item.description: The authorities on Sunday opened the Shailmari Sluice Gate at Dumuria in Khulna to facilitate the drainage of stagnant water from the waterlogged Beel Dakatia area, bringing relief to hundreds of thousands of residents affected by prolonged flooding....

item.title: Bangladesh joins BIMSTEC NSA meeting despite Delhi incident
item.description: Bangladesh will attend the National Security Advisers’ meeting of Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation in New Delhi on July 16 despite rejecting India’s explanation over the recent airport incident involving prime minister’s adviser Zahed Ur Rahman, foreign ministry’s officials said on Sunday....City& Local Guides

item.description: The country on Sunday launched a biomedical waste management facility in Sirajganj to ensure safe collection, treatment and disposal of healthcare waste, benefiting more than three million people while promoting environmental protection....

item.title: 11 customs officials sent to jail in graft case
item.description: The Dhaka Metropolitan Senior Special Judge Court on Sunday sent 11 serving and former customs officials to jail after rejecting their bail petitions in a case filed by the Anti-Corruption Commission over allegations of embezzling crores of taka through fake exports and misappropriating export incentives....

item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman is scheduled to visit Barishal today to take part in tree plantation programmes and exchange views with local BNP leaders and activists....

item.title: Creative economy needs strategic policy support
item.description: THE creative economy, or the ‘orange economy,’ an evolving knowledge-based system where creativity, cultural heritage and intellectual property act as primary drivers of value and economic growth, has so far been neglected as a mere cultural pursuit. But it has the potential to emerge as the next driver of growth, employment creation and exports only if the sector is......

item.title: Govt should take Mirpur water crisis as warning
item.description: SUPPLY water crisis at Mirpur is a warning of a larger crisis for Dhaka unless the government curbs the unsustainable reliance on groundwater, reverses its continued depletion and undertakes meaningful reforms to address governance failures of the authorities. Thousands of residents have suffered from an acute water shortage for two months while the Water Supply......InternationalSports Competitions

item.title: Next frontier for growth and jobs
item.description: Bangladesh stands at an important economic crossroads. For decades, its development story has been driven by agriculture, remittances and, most notably, apparel. These sectors have transformed the economy, reduced poverty and created employment opportunities for millions. Yet, as Bangladesh aspires to become a high-income country in the coming decades, it......

item.title: Population ageing demands action
item.description: BANGLADESH’S demographic dividend has contributed significantly to higher productivity, rapid urbanisation and poverty reduction. Beneath this demographic profile, however, lies a less discussed but increasingly urgent reality: the country is ageing faster than many anticipated. Failure to prepare for this demographic transition could create serious economic, social......

item.title: PROPOSED INDIGENOUS DEVELOPMENT DIRECTORATE: Positive step, with concerns
item.description: THE demand for establishing a separate government institution dedicated to the development needs and aspirations of the Indigenous (Adivasi) peoples of the plains regions in Bangladesh has existed for decades. The government’s current proposal to establish an Indigenous (Small Ethnic Communities) Development Directorate is, undoubtedly, a positive and......

item.title: Making of India’s push-in crisis
item.description: BANGLADESH has been facing a ‘push-in’ crisis along its border for over a year. Unlike legitimate deportation, a push-in is a forceful, extrajudicial expulsion that does not require any judicial verdict, citizenship verification, or bilateral diplomatic dialogue. The first recent case of push-in was recorded on May 7, 2025. Since then, many people living in India have been......Environmentclimate change

item.description: The government has no immediate plan to raise power prices, officials said on Monday....GeographicReference

item.description: The Bangladesh Bank has allowed foreign currency-taka swap facilities for exporters operating in specialised economic zones, allowing them to obtain short-term Taka liquidity by using idle foreign currency deposits without converting or withdrawing their export earnings......

item.title: Rupali Bank’s provision shortfall hits Tk 14,014cr
